timeline.label: H1 FY2026 revenue $3.63B (+8.5% YoY) and diluted EPS $2.27 signal continued recovery after biopharma softness
FY2025 net revenue $6.95B (+6.7%); operating income $1.48B; free cash flow ~$1.15B after $407M capex
Segment reorganization into Life Sciences and Diagnostics Markets, Agilent CrossLab, and Applied Markets; historical segment data recast
Closed $1.2B senior notes offering (2027 and 2034) to partially fund BIOVECTRA acquisition and term-loan prepayment
Agreed to acquire BIOVECTRA CDMO for $925M from H.I.G. Capital; closed before calendar 2025
Effective Nov 1, 2024 operating model shift: consumables/chemistries moved to CrossLab; instruments split LS&D vs Applied Markets
FY2023 revenue $6.83B peak before biopharma funding downturn pressured instrument placements
Acquired Resolution Bioscience to expand cancer diagnostics and NGS portfolio in diagnostics group
Spun off electronic measurement business as Keysight Technologies; Agilent focused on life sciences, diagnostics, and applied chemical analysis
IPO spin-off from Hewlett-Packard as independent Agilent Technologies

industry.structure: Global life-science tools market is oligopolistic among Agilent, Danaher, Thermo Fisher, and Waters; switching costs arise from validated lab workflows and recurring consumables on installed instruments.
industry.share_shift: Agilent gained share in LS&D (+11% FY2025 including BIOVECTRA) while Applied Markets grew only 1%; peers with broader bioprocessing exposure recovered faster from the 2023–2024 funding downturn.
industry.trend: Biopharma capital budgets are stabilizing in 2025–2026; CDMO/consolidation (BIOVECTRA) and CrossLab services mix shift revenue toward recurring streams; China and academic funding remain swing factors.
